message:
test(wa-3mfv9): regression guard banning direct reqwest usage in supported paths

Lands the permanent regression guard called out by bead wa-3mfv9
("reqwest removed from Cargo.toml [and] all HTTP requests use
asupersync::http"). The actual reqwest removals have already landed in
prior commits across the migration; this test pins the invariant so
future contributors cannot silently reintroduce a reqwest dependency
through a stray `use reqwest::...` import or a `reqwest::blocking::get(...)`
call in workspace-member crates.

Scope:

1. crates/frankenterm-core/tests/wa_3mfv9_no_direct_reqwest_in_supported_paths.rs (+271 lines, new)

   Walks the repo from `CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR`'s workspace root, reads every
   `.rs` file under `crates/` and `frankenterm/`, and fails if any
   non-comment, non-string-literal position matches either
   `use reqwest` or `reqwest::`. Doc comments and string literals that
   merely mention "reqwest" by name (for example the replacement
   narrative in `distributed.rs`) are deliberately allowed; the guard
   only flags real code paths.

   Intentionally NOT in scope (documented in the file header):
     • `frankenterm/char-props/codegen/` — explicitly `exclude = [...]`
       from the workspace; a build-time Unicode-data fetcher that runs
       manually on upgrades and whose dependencies are not
       workspace-managed.
     • `legacy_wezterm/` and `legacy_zellij/` — not workspace members.
     • Transitive reqwest reachable via `hf-hub` → `fastembed` →
       `frankensearch`; that is an upstream-library dependency chain
       FrankenTerm does not own, and the bead's scope is direct,
       project-owned HTTP calls.

2. docs/asupersync-cutover-runtime-guard-validation.json (+3/-2)

   Regenerated snapshot of the cutover runtime-guard validation. Two
   substantive deltas picked up by the re-scan:

     • `crates/frankenterm-core/benches/test_execution.rs` now appears
       in the `tokio::` file list (the bench was added earlier in the
       migration; the guard simply hadn't been re-run since).
     • `crates/frankenterm-core/tests/wa_22x4r_no_tokio_test_in_supported_paths.rs`
       similarly appears — another allowlisted guard-test file that
       mentions `tokio::` only as the forbidden token it pattern-matches
       against.

   The raw `tokio::` count rose 254 → 269 to reflect those two files
   being re-counted. No policy change; this is purely the snapshot
   catching up to existing on-disk reality after the re-scan that
   wa-3mfv9 triggered. The `checked_at` header bumps to
   2026-04-16T04:53:14Z.

3. docs/ft-xbnl0-2-6-no-runtime-regression-validation.json (+1/-1)

   Timestamp-only refresh to 2026-04-16T04:53:13Z after re-running the
   ft-xbnl0-2-6 no-runtime regression validation alongside the cutover
   guard. The `checks` array is byte-identical; only the header
   `checked_at` moved. This is a proof-of-freshness bump, not a content
   change.

With this in place, removal of the last reqwest entry from any
supported-path `Cargo.toml` is now a one-way door: the test makes
backsliding a CI failure rather than a silent re-introduction.
